title: Skeptics In Pulte Homes Next Bull Run May Get Burnt
text: The residential construction sector has seen a stratospheric rise during the past year and a half, with leading dynamics in the underlying economy fueling further demand for housing and further amplifying the need for construction. PulteGroup NYSE: PHM is one of the lucky names that rode the rally wave with still more upside potential than meets the eye.

title: 3 Housing Stocks to Avoid as Interest Rates Rise
text: On July 26, the Federal Reserve is likely to raise interest rates by 25 basis points (0.25%). As much hype as analysts put on the Federal Reserve's decision, the announcement itself tends to be a non-event.

title: D.R. Horton draws upgrade to outperform at Raymond James on rental business boost
text: D.R. Horton Inc. DHI, +1.72% stock was up 1.2% in premarket trading on Monday after Raymond James upgraded the home builder to outperform from market perform, with a price target of $160 a share. Raymond James also boosted the company's 2024 profit outlook by 25%, due to “an impressive rebound in homebuilding margins and a surging revenue contribution from its $3 billion rental housing portfolio,” said analyst Buck Horne.

title: You Can't Control the Housing Market, but You Can Control What You Do About It
text: Rising benchmark interest rates have pushed mortgage rates higher, increasing monthly and overall costs for borrowers. Tight housing supply has pushed the prices of existing inventory higher.

title: DR Horton had a strong Q3 but price action today has been ‘dangerous'
text: DR Horton Inc (NYSE: DHI) is in the red today even though it reported strong results for the fiscal third quarter. Pro reacts to the decline in share price The price action is all the more interesting considering the homebuilder also raised its guidance for the full year.

title: D.R. Horton (DHI) Tops Q3 Earnings & Revenues, Ups 2023 View
text: D.R. Horton's (DHI) fiscal third quarter reflects higher orders and improved demand despite higher mortgage rates and general economic uncertainty.

title: D.R. Horton (DHI) Q3 Earnings: Taking a Look at Key Metrics Versus Estimates (Revised)
text: Although the revenue and EPS for D.R. Horton (DHI) give a sense of how its business performed in the quarter ended June 2023, it might be worth considering how some key metrics compare with Wall Street estimates and the year-ago numbers.

title: D.R. Horton, Inc. (DHI) Q3 2023 Earnings Call Transcript
text: D.R. Horton, Inc. (NYSE:DHI ) Q3 2023 Earnings Conference Call July 19, 2023 8:30 AM ET Company Participants Jessica Hansen - Vice President of Investor Relations David Auld - President and Chief Executive Officer Paul Romanowski - Executive Vice President and Co-Chief Operating Officer Mike Murray - Executive Vice President and Co-Chief Operating Officer Bill Wheat - Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer Conference Call Participants Stephen Kim - Evercore ISI Joe Ahlersmeyer - Deutsche Bank John Lovallo - UBS Carl Reichardt - BTIG Mike Rehaut - JPMorgan Matthew Bouley - Barclays Eric Bosshard - Cleveland Research Company Ken Zener - Seaport Research Partners Mike Dahl - RBC Capital Alan Ratner - Zelman & Associates Truman Patterson - Wolfe Research Rafe Jadrosich - Bank of America Operator Good morning, and welcome to the Third Quarter 2023 Earnings Conference Call for D.R. Horton, America's Builder, the largest builder in the United States.

title: Is This The Top For D.R. Horton Stock?
text: As dubious as the signals within the housing market, stocks like D.R. Horton NYSE: DHI have been rallying to new highs.

title: Will a Decrease in Housing Starts Hurt Homebuilder Stocks?
text: Homebuilders traded lower as a group on July 19, ending the session down 0.20% after the National Association of Homebuilders reported that June housing starts fell more than expected.

title: D.R. Horton (DHI) Q3 Earnings and Revenues Surpass Estimates
text: D.R. Horton (DHI) came out with quarterly earnings of $3.90 per share, beating the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $2.82 per share. This compares to earnings of $4.67 per share a year ago.

title: D.R. Horton stock builds momentum on 3Q earnings beat
text: D.R. Horton (NYSE:DHI) shares moved higher in pre-market trading after the home builder's third-quarter fiscal 2023 earnings topped expectations.

title: D.R. Horton's stock surges into record territory after bit profit and revenue beats, raised outlook
text: Shares of D.R. Horton Inc. DHI, -0.02% shot up 6.4% toward a record high in premarket trading Thursday, after the homebuilder reported a big profit and revenue beat for the fiscal third quarter and raised its full-year outlook, as supply of affordable existing homes remains limited.

title: D.R. Horton, Inc., America's Builder, Reports Fiscal 2023 Third Quarter Earnings and Declares Quarterly Dividend of $0.25 Per Share
text: ARLINGTON, Texas--(BUSINESS WIRE)---- $DHI #earnings--D.R. Horton reported that net income for its third fiscal quarter ended June 30, 2023 was $3.90 per diluted share.

title: D.R. Horton Reports Earnings Soon. What to Watch For.
text: The home builder is expected to report earnings of $2.83 a share on revenue of about $8.3 billion for its June quarter.

title: 3 Housing Stocks to Avoid as Fed Looks to Raise Rates
text: If you'll excuse the pandering to the SEO algorithm overlords, investors must start seriously considering the impact of interest rates on housing stocks to avoid. Basically, all eyes center on the Federal Reserve.

title: D.R. Horton (DHI) to Report Q3 Earnings: What to Expect?
text: D.R. Horton's (DHI) fiscal Q3 results are likely to have reflected sequentially higher revenues, given more demand for new homes due to a lack of existing homes for sale. Yet, higher expenses are a concern.

title: Analysts Estimate D.R. Horton (DHI) to Report a Decline in Earnings: What to Look Out for
text: D.R. Horton (DHI) doesn't possess the right combination of the two key ingredients for a likely earnings beat in its upcoming report. Get prepared with the key expectations.

title: Housing Demand Gives Home Builders a Solid Foundation for More Gains
text: Home-builder stocks D.R. Horton, Lennar, and NVR are up between 29% and 33% this year, well above the S&P 500's 13% gain.

title: Home-builder stocks take a broad beating as 10-year yield's breakout fuels demand concerns
text: The home-builder sector suffered a unanimous selloff Thursday, as a jump in Treasury yields on the back of strong jobs data sparked concerns that higher mortgage rates would hurt demand for new homes.

title: Here's Why Homebuilder Stocks D.R. Horton Climbed 14% and Lennar Rose 17% in June
text: Lennar's quarterly results topped analyst forecasts, and investors were impressed with the company's outlook. Housing-starts data and the Fed's pause on interest rate hikes made investors bullish about the sector.

title: D.R. Horton (DHI) Buys Truland Homes, Boosts Gulf Coast Presence
text: D.R. Horton (DHI) strengthens its Gulf Coast dominance with the acquisition of Truland Homes, unlocking growth opportunities and expanding its market presence.

title: Yield Curve Tests New Lows, Where Markets Are Seeking Safety
text: As markets come to a significant turning point, investors can benefit from understanding where money has been and where it may be going into, as pivots in the flow of investment preferences usually give a window of opportunity for those who can spot it. In today's environment, some instruments and indicators give out 'warning' signs, causing broader allocators to rotate their portfolios to maneuver around changing tides.

title: D.R. Horton, Inc. Acquires the Homebuilding Operations of Truland Homes
text: ARLINGTON, Texas--(BUSINESS WIRE)---- $DHI #acquisition--D.R. Horton, Inc. announced the acquisition of Truland Homes, the largest private homebuilder along the Gulf Coast.
